incident.definition: An unplanned interruption to a service or a reduction in quality. Something is broken or degraded.

service_request.definition: A standard, pre-approved request for access, information, software, equipment, or onboarding/offboarding work.

incident_vs_request.rule_of_thumb: If a service was working and now is not, it is an Incident. If the user is asking for something that is normal and approved, it is a Request.

triage_flow.name: Validate caller and contact method
questions: Is the caller authorized for this request?

triage_flow.name: Confirm scope
questions: One user, one device, one location, or many?
Is there a duplicate incident pattern?

triage_flow.name: Set impact, urgency, priority
questions: How many users or services are affected?

triage_flow.name: Troubleshoot or escalate
questions: What evidence does the next team need?
Did you add clear work notes?

triage_flow.name: Communicate and close
questions: Does the resolution note state cause, action, and confirmation?

priority_matrix.use_when: Major outage, security incident, production service down, payroll/finance/patient-safety service stopped
Multiple users affected, degraded critical service, major VIP/business deadline risk
Single user cannot work, account lockout, standard access issue
Single-user workaround exists, peripheral issue, non-urgent hardware/software problem
Approved install, how-to, request/fulfillment, scheduled non-break/fix work

routing_matrix.route_when: Normal account unlock/password reset after identity verification
routing_matrix.escalate_when: VIP compromise, impossible travel, lost MFA token, suspicious activity, repeated lockouts after reset

routing_matrix.route_when: Outlook client issue, one mailbox, profile/add-in problem
routing_matrix.escalate_when: Mail flow delay, multiple users, Exchange/O365 service health issue

routing_matrix.route_when: Windows update, disk space, driver, local OS issue
routing_matrix.escalate_when: Fleet-wide BSOD, Intune/SCCM package issue, BitLocker recovery loops

routing_matrix.route_when: Single endpoint or peripheral hardware issue
routing_matrix.escalate_when: Multiple devices after update, warranty replacement, repeated failures
Multi-room outage, Teams Rooms platform issue

routing_matrix.route_when: Single-user Wi-Fi profile or credential issue
routing_matrix.escalate_when: Many users, floor/room outage, AP issue, certificate problem
Multiple remote users, gateway/client update issue, certificate failure
Routing, packet loss, data center, firewall, WAN, DNS records out of date

routing_matrix.route_when: Query timeout, backup failure, replication lag, DB alert
routing_matrix.escalate_when: Application code defect plus DB symptom

routing_matrix.route_when: One endpoint has stale definitions or scan needed
routing_matrix.escalate_when: Malware, ransomware, multiple endpoints, suspicious encryption, Defender alerts

routing_matrix.route_when: Reported phishing, suspicious email, MFA anomaly, suspicious sign-in
routing_matrix.escalate_when: Confirmed compromise, multi-user phishing wave, terminated account still active

routing_matrix.route_when: Known app issue or user-specific app troubleshooting
routing_matrix.escalate_when: HTTP 500, SSO/SAML, workflow break, production release regression, payroll/HRIS impact
routing_matrix.escalation_group: Specific app team / IAM / ServiceNow Platform Team

routing_matrix.route_when: License assignment, group membership, mailbox permissions, SSO config
routing_matrix.escalate_when: Suspected compromise, terminated user still active, mass deprovisioning required

work_note_templates.initial_triage: Validated caller: <method>. Scope: <one user/device/location or multiple>. Symptoms: <exact error>. Business impact: <impact>. Troubleshooting started: <steps>.
work_note_templates.tier1_resolution: Validated caller identity. Issue was limited to one user/device. Confirmed symptoms: <symptom>. Troubleshooting completed: <steps>. Result: <test result>. User confirmed service is restored. Closing as resolved.
work_note_templates.escalation: Validated caller identity. Confirmed scope: <number affected/users/services/location>. Troubleshooting completed: <steps>. Evidence collected: <error/log/screenshot/timestamp>. Business impact: <impact>. Escalating to <assignment group> because <reason group owns fix>.
work_note_templates.outage_parent: Found multiple related incidents with same symptoms: <incident numbers>. Affected service/location: <service/location>. Current impact: <number affected>. Linked this ticket to parent incident <parent>. Escalating for outage review.
work_note_templates.security: Validated reporter. User did not click links/open attachment: <yes/no/unknown>. Collected sender, subject, timestamp, and attachment/link details. Instructed user not to interact with message. Escalating to Security Operations for review.

customer_comment_templates.initial_response: We received your ticket and are reviewing the issue. I will check the affected service, document the findings, and update you with the next step.
customer_comment_templates.need_info: Please send the device name, screenshot of the error, and whether anyone else near you is experiencing the same issue.
customer_comment_templates.escalated: I found that this issue needs a specialist team. I have escalated the ticket with the troubleshooting details and will keep the ticket updated as work continues.
customer_comment_templates.resolved: The issue should now be resolved. Please test again and reply if the problem returns.

work_notes_vs_comments.work_notes: Internal-only journal. Contains exact errors, log lines, screenshots, IPs, hostnames, troubleshooting steps, evidence, names of related incidents, and the reason for the next action. Reviewers and the next team read this.
work_notes_vs_comments.customer_comments: User-visible. Plain language. No internal tool names, no blame, no uncertain root cause, no security details the user does not need. Acknowledge, set expectation, confirm next step.

escalation_triggers: Three or more tickets with the same short description, service, or CI
Multiple users, rooms, floors, departments, or locations affected
Security concern: phishing, malware, ransomware, lost MFA token, suspicious login, MFA prompt from another country/state
Production database, ERP, payroll/HRIS, email, VPN, network, or identity service degraded
HTTP 500, SQL deadlock, Kerberos/SPN failure, packet loss, DHCP scope exhaustion, DNS stale record
A recent change, deployment, update, or maintenance window lines up with symptoms
Tier 1 fix attempted but issue persists and logs/error codes are collected
Account lockout repeats after a successful reset (lockout source unknown)
BitLocker recovery loop with no recovery key on file
Defender / EDR quarantine alert on an endpoint

major_incident.what: A major incident has broad business impact (many users, critical service down, regulatory/financial deadline at risk). It runs on a separate workflow from a normal incident.
how_to_recognize: Multiple incoming tickets with same symptom
Security event with potential data loss
what_to_do: Stop individual troubleshooting once outage is suspected
Open or link a parent (major) incident
Notify the Incident Commander / on-call manager
Communicate scope and ETA to stakeholders on a regular cadence
Capture timeline for the post-incident review

problem_management.definition: Problem Management identifies and removes the root cause of recurring incidents. Incident Management restores service. Problem Management prevents the next outage.
when_to_open_problem: Same incident recurring on the same CI or service
Workaround exists but cause is unknown
Patterns found across many small tickets

ticket_field_design.principle: Use dropdown fields for category, subcategory, impact, urgency, priority, assignment group, and channel. Free-text is for description, work notes, and resolution only.
ticket_field_design.why: Dropdowns make tickets routable, reportable, and consistent. Free-text in classification fields breaks routing and metrics.

password_ad.title: Password / Active Directory
password_ad.when_to_use: Account lockouts, expired passwords, repeated authentication failures
steps: Verify caller identity (employee ID + callback or manager confirmation)
Check AD: account status, bad password count, last lockout time, lockout source DC
Unlock account
Reset password and force change at next login if needed
Confirm caller can sign in (Windows + email)
If lockout repeats within 1 hour, escalate to IAM with lockout source

outlook_email.title: Outlook / Email client
outlook_email.when_to_use: Outlook crashes, hangs, missing folders, profile errors
steps: Start Outlook in safe mode: outlook.exe /safe
Disable COM add-ins one at a time
Repair the Office installation (Quick Repair, then Online Repair)
Recreate the Outlook profile (Mail applet > Show Profiles)
Repair the OST/PST (scanpst.exe) only if data integrity warning appears
Test in OWA to isolate client vs mailbox issue
escalate_when: Mailbox not accessible from OWA either

printer_spooler.title: Printer / Print Spooler
printer_spooler.when_to_use: Stuck print queue, single user cannot print, repeated spooler crashes
steps: Cancel all stuck jobs in the queue
Stop Print Spooler service: net stop spooler
Clear spool folder: %WINDIR%\System32\spool\PRINTERS\*
Start Print Spooler service: net start spooler
Power cycle the printer (off, 30s, on)
Verify driver version matches the print server
Send test page
escalate_when: Multiple printers offline on same floor
Spooler crashes after a recent driver rollout

vpn_wifi.title: VPN / Wi-Fi
vpn_wifi.when_to_use: Connection failures, repeated disconnects, certificate errors, slow Wi-Fi
steps: Confirm SSID and credentials match the corporate profile
Forget and rejoin the wireless network
Reset network adapter (disable/enable)
Check VPN client version and certificate validity
Test wired connection to isolate Wi-Fi vs identity vs gateway
ipconfig /all, ping default gateway, ping VPN endpoint

dns_dhcp.title: DNS / DHCP
dns_dhcp.when_to_use: Cannot resolve hostnames, no IP address received, stale records after migration
steps: ipconfig /all to confirm DHCP-assigned IP, subnet, gateway, DNS servers
ipconfig /flushdns to clear local cache
nslookup <hostname> against local and authoritative DNS
ping <hostname> and ping <ip> to isolate DNS vs reachability
netsh int ip reset and netsh winsock reset on the client if local stack is suspect
escalate_when: DHCP scope exhausted on a VLAN
DNS returns old IP after a migration

intune_device.title: Intune / Device Management
intune_device.when_to_use: App install failures from Company Portal, compliance failures, BitLocker recovery loops
steps: Confirm device check-in time in Intune
Review app install error code and target device(s)
Sync device from Settings > Accounts > Access work or school
Check BitLocker recovery key in the IAM portal
Verify TPM/BIOS state if recovery loop persists
escalate_when: Same install error on multiple devices
Compliance regression after a policy change

security_phishing.title: Security / Phishing
security_phishing.when_to_use: Reported phishing, suspicious MFA prompts, possible compromise, terminated account still active
steps: Tell the caller NOT to click, reply, or forward
Collect sender, subject, timestamp, headers if available, attachment or URL
Check whether other users received the same message
For MFA anomalies: check sign-in location, disable sessions, force password reset, notify SecOps
For terminated user: confirm with HR, disable account, revoke tokens, notify manager
Escalate to Security Operations with all evidence
escalate_when: Always escalate. Tier 1 collects and hands off; Tier 1 does not investigate phishing alone.

database_erp.title: Database / ERP
database_erp.when_to_use: HTTP 500 errors, SQL deadlocks, replication lag, backup failures
steps: Capture exact error, screenshot, timestamp, user ID, app module
Check whether other users hit the same error
Request a HAR file or server-side log reference if available
Note any recent release/deploy/maintenance window
Escalate to the application team plus DBA if symptom is in DB
escalate_when: Always escalate to the application team. DBAs join when DB error or performance metric is in scope.

windows_network.what: Show all adapters, DHCP server, DNS, IP, subnet, gateway
Path to a host with per-hop latency

windows_print.what: Clear stuck print jobs from spool folder

windows_outlook.what: Start Outlook in safe mode (no add-ins)

closure_checklist: Impact, urgency, and priority match scope
Category/subcategory and assignment group are correct
Work note includes symptoms, evidence, actions, and outcome
Caller comment is plain and not overly technical
Resolution note says what was fixed and how it was verified
Related incidents or parent incident linked when applicable
Knowledge article updated or created if this was a new pattern

scoring_keywords.description: Strong work notes mention each of these concepts. The simulator scores keyword coverage on every ticket.

coaching_tips: Do not escalate first. Validate caller, define scope, collect evidence, then escalate.
One user usually starts Tier 1. Multiple users, same service, same location, or same error usually means escalation or outage review.
Priority is not based on who is loudest. Priority comes from impact and urgency.

The assignment group should own the fix. Service Desk owns basic troubleshooting, Desktop Support owns endpoint/device, Network owns connectivity and DHCP/DNS, IAM owns access and authentication, Security owns phishing/malware/suspicious sign-ins, DBA owns database performance.
Work notes are internal proof. Explain what was checked, what was found, what changed, and why the next action makes sense.
Caller comments are plain language. No internal tool names, no blame, no uncertain root cause, no security details the user does not need.
Three or more similar tickets should trigger related-incident checking, parent-incident linking, and possibly a major-incident workflow.
Always tell phishing reporters: do not click, do not reply, do not forward. Collect evidence and escalate to Security Operations.
Lockouts that repeat after a successful reset mean the lockout source is unknown. Escalate to IAM with the source DC.
BitLocker recovery loops with no key on file are not Tier 1 fixes. Pull the asset tag and escalate to Desktop Support.
